place: Hamburg borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Around 1770, settler Joseph Sharp built the Sharpsboro Iron Works, a mill and forge that briefly renamed the town Sharpsboro.
In 1753 Sussex County was formed, and Hamburg became part of the newly established Hardyston Township in 1762.
The Hamburg Concert Band, founded in 1888, has hosted weekly summer concerts that became a staple of community life.
A historic bandstand sits at the center of town where Routes 23 and 94 intersect, serving as a long‑standing landmark.
Colonel Joseph Edsall, a local forge owner, was elected to the U.S. Congress in 1847, a rare political achievement for a small town.
Hamburg is governed under New Jersey’s borough form of municipal government, the most common structure among the state’s 564 municipalities.
